---
title: "Sally Tsai"
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 15em
  image-shape: round
  image: photos/59AF9C00-B7DF-407C-B74A-678C40F349BF_1_102_o.jpeg
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: "Email"
      href: "mailto:sallytsai6640@gmail.com"
    - icon: github
      text: "GitHub"
      href: https://github.com/sallyytsai
    - icon: linkedin
      text: "LinkedIn"
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sallyytsai/
---

I am a **Master in Public Administration in International Development (MPA/ID)** student at the **Harvard Kennedy School**, specializing in **political economy**, **quantitative policy analysis**, and **international development**. My research focuses on how security concerns, industrial policy, and political institutions shape economic outcomes — with a particular interest in East Asia and cross-strait relations.

My work combines rigorous empirical methods — causal inference, ordered logistic regression, synthetic control — with deep engagement in the political and institutional context behind the numbers. I have applied these tools to questions ranging from Taiwan's EV trade restrictions to Brazil's health infrastructure to fiscal policy in emerging markets.

I am especially interested in roles at the intersection of data-driven analysis and policy design, in government, multilateral institutions, or applied research.

---

## Featured Projects

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Taiwan EV Policy & Cross-Strait Security
**Methods:** Ordered logit · Synthetic control · Placebo inference

Why does Taiwan maintain the world's most restrictive policy toward Chinese electric vehicles? This paper argues security salience — not industrial lobbying — is the primary driver, using cross-national data from 60+ countries.

[View paper →](pdfs/taiwan-ev-policy-analysis.pdf)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Brazil Health Outcomes
**Methods:** Quantitative analysis · Program evaluation

Examining the relationship between public health infrastructure and outcomes across Brazilian municipalities.

[View paper →](pdfs/brazil-health-outcome.pdf)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Growth Decomposition: Azerbaijan vs Turkey
**Methods:** Growth accounting · Policy memo

Comparative macroeconomic analysis of growth drivers for the Asian Development Bank.

[View paper →](pdfs/Asian Development Bank Internal Memorandum.pdf)
:::

:::

---

## Skills & Tools

**Programming & Tools:** R (tidyverse, ggplot2, knitr, Quarto), Excel, Claude AI, ChatGPT

**Methods:** Causal inference (IV, RD, DiD) · Ordered logistic regression · Synthetic control · OLS with robust SEs · Growth accounting · Political economy analysis

**Areas:** Cross-strait political economy · Industrial policy · Social protection · State capacity · Fiscal policy in emerging markets
